counter_case: Renaming a placeholder string to avoid a Snyk false positive is cosmetic. The field is documentation-only and poses zero security risk. Users who understand the tool will know this is a placeholder. Changing placeholder names to satisfy scanners is a slippery slope.
summary: The fix is trivial (rename a placeholder string) with zero risk. While it is a false positive from Snyk, eliminating scanner noise is valuable for users who run automated security scans on their dependencies. Cannot build a strong case against a zero-risk rename.
